title: cytisus hirsutus var. scopolii
intro: Cytisus hirsutus var. scopolii, also known as Scopolia or Scopolia leaves, is a plant species that belongs to the Fabaceae family, commonly referred to as a type of broom or a wild broom. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include containing alkaloids such as hyoscyamine and scopolamine that have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Cytisus hirsutus var. scopolii include relief from muscle spasms, digestive issues, and anxiety, as well as its use in treating respiratory problems and as a sedative. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used in teas, tinctures, and infusions, though its use should be approached with caution due to potential side effects and interactions with other medications.
